text:
14 NOVEMBER 1960

I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC

II. ASIA-AFRICA
Situation
token_text: 14 november 1960 i the communist bloc ii asia-africa situation

text:
South Vietnam. (1)
Laos--Coup group in Luang Prabang establishing ties with Phoumi's committee in Savannakhet. (2)
Thailand--Discontented junior officers, apprehensive over moves to improve Thai-Soviet relations, allegedly planning coup against Premier Sarit. (3)
Turkey--Shake-up in ruling military group appears to have strengthened hand of moderate pro-Western elements. (4)

III. THE WEST
West Germany--Adenauer trying to improve relations with USSR but the prospect of a Bonn-Moscow "deal" is discounted. (5)
Venezuela--Betancourt to exclude pro-Castro URD from reorganized cabinet. (6)
Nicaragua--Claims that Castro regime directed recent rebel incursion from Costa Rica remain unconfirmed; government expects attack from the north. (7)
Guatemalan insurgents attack key military installations; regime decrees state of siege throughout country. (8

text:
to obtain a no-confidence vote against Souvanna. The majority of deputies, although anxious for an end to the three-month-old crisis, are probably too fearful of retaliation by Captain Kong Le and the Pathet Lao to risk unseating Souvanna.

Quinim Pholsena, Souvanna's leftist information minister, mission to Sam Neua was a failure. the Pathet Lao were unwilling to turn over civil administration of the province to the royal government, although they might eventually release five Laotian army and police officers they have in custody.

the impression that Prince Souphannouvong, titular leader of the Pathet Lao movement, was not a free agent and possessed little power for independent decision. Souphannouvong's seeming lack of confidence and his evasiveness when substantive issues were discussed

text: USSR for a new trade agreement as long as possible in the expectation that Moscow will meanwhile allow no new moves against the city. A cabinet member is reported to have stated that Bonn plans to retract its cancellation of the trade agreement with East Germany before its scheduled lapse on 31 December. In any event, Foreign Minister von Brentano has indicated that West Germany will try to buy some time by allowing trade with East Germany to continue indefinitely so long as there is no further interference with traffic to Berlin
